channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Yellow lines in poor condition, 3rd June
item.description:
The yellow lines on the junction of Whaddon road and Severn Road, Cheltenham are in extremely poor condition. In some places almost no existent.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 6, Severn Road, Lynworth, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large craters! Road is a disgrace, 3rd June
item.description:
Massive craters on this road. Absolute disgrace. Nothing has been done! What are we paying our taxes for? Whole road needs resurfacing!
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 30, Hatherley Road, Warden Hill, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: TMC - Defects -other CW10 problem, 3rd June
item.description:
Cw depression 2000x400x40mm approx 3 way stop and go (CW10 Rutting&Depressions)
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Post or sign leaning slightly (less than 20 degrees) problem, 3rd June
item.description:
I’m hoping we can arrange a change of signage for our end of Cold Slad lane. At the moment the sign indicates a dead end which is appropriate. Unfortunately it also displays a sign ’Not suitable for motor vechicles’ The lane is clearly for motor vehicle use and our house sits at the far end of the lane. We drive it every day and have recently had many trucks and lorries use it successfully as there is plenty of turning space at the end of the lane. This sign is potentially dangerous as in the case of needing emergency services vehicles to come down the lane it could potentially be misleading. The sign also seems to prevents the council from keeping the lane under proper maintenance and we are constantly reporting potholes due to its neglect. It would be a simple matter to change the sign and we are happy to do it ourselves. Are you able to help us with this query or potentially put us in touch with someone who could? Thanks
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Post or sign leaning slightly (less than 20 degrees)

item.title: Significant rain water pooling penetrating to vault below housing electric and gas supplies, 3rd June
item.description:
At the junction of road and path directly in front of 1 Bath Street, GL50 1YE - I have emailed you separately a full account - 6 months +

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 32, Bath Street, Ewens Farm, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Other damage to pavement i.e. cobbles, raised mound/surface

item.title: Green or amber traffic light is not working problem, 3rd June
item.description:
Whole junction is out completely including pedestrian lights.
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Green or amber traffic light is not working

item.title: Green or amber traffic light is not working problem, 3rd June
item.description:
Customer has driven through this signaled junction at 12:15. As they approached all of the lights went out - no illumination at all.
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Green or amber traffic light is not working

item.title: Obstructed view when turning right out of Hartley Lane onto Leckhampton Hill, 3rd June
item.description:
The vegetation and grass verge which runs alongside the sub station on Leckhampton Hill is overgrown and is obstructing the view when you are trying to turn right out of Hartley Lane to head down Leckhampton Hill. The uploaded photo demoonstrates how close the oncoming traffic is before you get a clear view. We live at Firsbrake and constantly hear car horns as people are turning out into the road infront of other cars. Please can the vegetation and verge be cut back as much as possible all along the way opposite Banavie and Greenhill to allow drivers to have as much a view as possible of the oncoming traffic, thank you.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Leckhampton Hill, Shurdington, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: TMC - Defects - Other FW6 Surface problem, 3rd June
item.description:
FW: Abrupt Level Difference 800mmx5400mmx22mm TM: Give and Take Stats required Outside No 22 (OWNERS WILL HAVE TO MOVE THEIR CAR). (FW6 Abrupt Level Differences)
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Post or sign leaning slightly (less than 20 degrees) problem, 3rd June
item.description:
Faded NS limit sign CW. Please pass on to the relevant AHR to arrange reinstatement
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Post or sign leaning slightly (less than 20 degrees)

item.title: Street name isn't readable, 3rd June
item.description:
"Eldersfield Close" street name sign is unreadable - basically a plain white sign with black border. Naming on the plate is raised, not flat. Possibly the original sign from when the Close was built?
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Eldersfield Close, Winchcombe, Winchcombe, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 2 x Potholes, 3rd June
item.description:
2 x potholes adjanct to each other.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 6, Jersey Avenue, Ewens Farm, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: TMC - Defects - Kerbs and Channels problem, 3rd June
item.description:

Report on FixMyStreet
